steps.title: Find the author's point
steps.content: The passage starts with Joplin's famous works, then shifts with however to claim that overlooking his less famous works means missing his full creativity. It ends by saying those works deserve as much fame as his biggest hits.

steps.title: Separate purpose from detail
steps.content: Choice B focuses on ragtime and opera, which are only examples. Choice C broadens the purpose to many composers, but the passage is only about Joplin. Choice D discusses how Joplin learned music, which the passage never explains. The remaining choice captures the argument for more attention to lesser-known works. The answer is A.
